name: Pasta Fagioli (Pasta and Bean Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Northern white beans; soak overnight
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow pan, cover 1 pound Northern white beans with cold water by 2 inches above beans. Let sit overnight.
2. Next day: Drain and rinse the beans. Cover them again with cold water in a pot that can be used on the stove top and in the oven. Bring to a moderate boil on the stove top.
3. Place the pot in a preheated oven at 325 degrees for 1 hour, covered. Keep in liquid until ready to use.
4. In a large pot, saute 1/2 cup chopped onions in 1/3 cup olive oil until pale yellow, on medium heat.
5. Add 2/3 cup chopped celery and 3/4 pound chopped ham. Cook for 10 minutes.
6. Then add the mixture to the pot with beans (reserve 2 cups of beans to mash) and cook for 20-25 minutes.
7. Bring to a boil. Mash the reserved 2 cups of beans and then add this to the soup.
8. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons basil, 3 teaspoons Italian seasoning, 3-4 minced garlic cloves, and 1 1/2 teaspoons black pepper.
9. When the soup boils, add the small shells pasta until al dente (it still has a "bite" to it, not too soft).
10. Serve in bowls along with a salad and garlic bread for dinner or alone with crackers for luncheon. This can be frozen.
